ddls:
CREATE TABLE "ads" (
  "ad_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each ad */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the category of the ad */,
  "ad_text" TEXT /* Text of the ad */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the ad is being promoted */,
  "state" TEXT /* State where the ad is being promoted */,
  "zip" INTEGER /* Zip code where the ad is being promoted */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the ad is being promoted */,
  "client_id" INTEGER /* ID of the client who owns the ad */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date when the ad starts being displayed */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* Date when the ad stops being displayed */,
  "budget" TEXT /* Budget allocated for the ad campaign */,
  "target_audience" TEXT /* Description of the target audience */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the ad (e.g., active, paused, completed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ad_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ads_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES categories ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ads_client_id FOREIGN KEY ("client_id") REFERENCES clients ("client_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "parent_category_id" INTEGER /* Reference to a parent category for hierarchical categorization */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_categories_parent_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_category_id") REFERENCES categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "clients" (
  "client_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each client */,
  "client_name" TEXT /* Name of the client */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the client */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the client */,
  "address" TEXT /* Physical address of the client */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry to which the client belongs */,
  "contact_person" TEXT /* Name of the primary contact person */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("client_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "services" (
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service */,
  "client_id" INTEGER /* ID of the client who offers the service */,
  "service_name" TEXT /* Name of the service */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the service */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the category of the service */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date when the service starts being offered */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* Date when the service stops being offered */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the service (e.g., active, discontinued) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("service_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_services_client_id FOREIGN KEY ("client_id") REFERENCES clients ("client_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_services_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES service_categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "parent_category_id" INTEGER /* Reference to a parent category for hierarchical categorization */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_categories_parent_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_category_id") REFERENCES service_categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_ads" (
  "service_ad_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service ad */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service associated with the ad */,
  "ad_id" INTEGER /* ID of the ad associated with the service */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("service_ad_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_ads_ad_id FOREIGN KEY ("ad_id") REFERENCES ads ("ad_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_ads_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ad_performance" (
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "ad_id" INTEGER /* ID of the ad associated with the metric */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric (e.g., clicks, views) */,
  "value" INTEGER /* Value of the metric */,
  "date_recorded" TEXT /* Date when the performance metric was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ad_performance_ad_id FOREIGN KEY ("ad_id") REFERENCES ads ("ad_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, client) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_ads" (
  "user_ad_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user ad */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who accessed the ad */,
  "ad_id" INTEGER /* ID of the ad accessed by the user */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the ad was accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Time when the ad was accessed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_ad_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_ads_ad_id FOREIGN KEY ("ad_id") REFERENCES ads ("ad_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_ads_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_services" (
  "user_service_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user service */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who accessed the service */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service accessed by the user */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the service was accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Time when the service was accessed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_service_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_services_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_services_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ad_updates" (
  "update_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each update */,
  "ad_id" INTEGER /* ID of the ad that was updated */,
  "update_date" TEXT /* Date when the update was made */,
  "update_type" TEXT /* Type of update (e.g., ad text, targeting) */,
  "update_value" TEXT /* Value of the update */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("update_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ad_updates_updated_by FOREIGN KEY ("updated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ad_updates_ad_id FOREIGN KEY ("ad_id") REFERENCES ads ("ad_id")
)
